channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Conan Exiles Enhanced Will Give You More Control Over Your Junk
item.content:encoded:
That’s right, that’s exactly what I meant by that title. Funcom has just unveiled Conan Exiles Enhanced, a gargantuan overhaul to the base game that’s coming for free on May 5th to all existing owners on Steam. It’s a monumental thing that upgrades the game to UE5, enhances the base UI, introduces new building mechanics, and much more.

It also brings a major feature that’s dubbed ‘Enhanced Endowment Slider.’ If you’re at all familiar with Conan Exiles, the nearly ten-year-old survival title, you’ll know what the team means by that…

Bigger, Better Weapons in Conan Exiles Enhanced

Now, let me preface this by saying that the press release for Conan Exiles Enhanced doesn’t explain what the Enhanced Endowment Slider is. Therefore, it’s up to us to imagine exactly what that could mean.

It’s worth stressing that the entire release and mood board for the Enhanced upgrade is super serious, talking about the wonderful overhaul to the game’s base make-up, the new UI, the enhanced engine, and so on, and then in the corner, you’ve got penises.

Even the huge FAQ for the upgrade doesn’t mention the new slider feature. We’re truly being kept in the dark about this until May 5th, when the update arrives.

In case you’re unfamiliar with Conan Exiles, I’ll explain how it’s a brutal and unabashed game that doesn’t sugarcoat anything about the harsh world you’re forced to survive in. You start your journey buck naked, and when crafting your character, you get to choose how well-equipped you are in the most intimate way possible.

It looks like Funcom has expanded on that concept, introducing the Enhanced Endowment Slider. In the small image that occupied the top corner of the mood board, all we can see is something rather large, obscured by a black box.

Is it something artistic? Are we talking deeper customization, or controlling the angles of things? Is this like Cyberpunk 2077, and we’re about to have artisanal pubic hair? The mind boggles.

It’s worth pointing out that not everything is getting bigger in Conan Exiles Enhanced. The base game’s install size has been slashed by two-thirds, going from 120 GB down to a measly 40 GB. How ironic!

What do you think the Enhanced Endowment Slider is all about? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Crimson Desert now has an easy mode

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Easyhoon Returns to T1 As Part of LCK Coaching Staff
item.content:encoded:
Lee ‘Easyhoon’ Ji-hoon has returned to T1, but this time he won’t be replacing Lee ‘Faker’ Sang-hyeok in the mid lane.

Over 10 years since he shared the role with Faker and lifted the Summoner’s Cup, Easyhoon has returned to T1 as a League of Legends coach.

Back then, Easyhoon got a lot of playtime as Faker was criticised for specifically not being good enough at Azir.

This makes the timing of Easyhoon’s return ironic, as Faker faces similar criticism again following poor performances on Aurora.

But Easyhoon’s playing days are behind him, as the official statement from T1 read:

“We’re excited to welcome ‘Easyhoon’ as the newest coach of T1 starting from Round 2 of the 2026 LCK. With his years of experience, we look forward to the leadership and guidance he will bring to the team.”

This could be a crucial pickup as six-time LoL World Champions T1 are struggling in the LCK this year.

T1 currently find themselves in sixth place with three wins and three losses, and don’t appear to be in good enough form to qualify for MSI or the Esports World Cup.

T1’s Current Coaching Set Up

After retiring as a player in 2023, Easyhoon has found recent success in coaching with BiliBili Gaming.

He joined BLG in 2024 and has helped the organisation win numerous titles, including its first international trophy at First Stand earlier this year.

Reinforcing the coaching staff was a priority for T1 to fill the gap left by Kim ‘kkOma’ Jeong-gyun.

kk0ma stepped down as T1 coach earlier this year, taking a temporary leave of absence due to personal reasons.

That left Im ‘Tom’ Jae-hyeon as interim head coach with help from legendary support Cho ‘Mata’ Se-hyeong.

Easyhoon already knows his fellow coaches well. Tom was his jungler at T1 for part of 2025, and he played with Mata on MVP Blue in 2023.

Do you expect T1 to turn things around again in time for LoL Worlds? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And in other retired player news, popular Twitch streamer Marc ‘Caedrel’ Lamont has declined the chance to play for Great Britain at the Esports Nations Cup.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Conan Exiles Enhanced Announced, Coming May 5th For Free
item.content:encoded:
Funcom has unveiled Conan Exiles Enhanced, a super-sized overhaul-based update coming to the existing platform for free on May 5th. The team confirmed that the update is arriving in time for the game’s eighth anniversary on May 8th, and it offers ‘dramatically improved visuals, enhanced performance, and modern rendering technology.’

Conan Exiles is a brutal and enormous open-world survival game that, even after almost a decade on the market, enjoys an active audience represented by thousands of concurrent players.

Conan Exiles is Getting Better Than Ever with Enhanced

In a press release, the CCO at Funcom, Joel Bylos, said:

Conan has been the heart of Funcom for nearly two decades, and with the new Enhanced upgrade, Conan Exiles continues to carve its legacy in video games.

We know these upgrades are often sold at a premium or released as a separate, enhanced product, but we wanted to offer this for free to fans past, present, and future, as an anniversary gift and a token of our appreciation for their support.

We are so proud of this game and the work our team has put into it over the years, and we want this upgrade to breathe new life and energy into the game for a long time to come.

The new ‘version’ of Conan Exiles has been compiled in collaboration with Inflexion Games, which also spoke on the impending arrival of the overhaul, which takes the game to the realms of Unreal Engine 5:

We’re honored to collaborate with Funcom on upgrading Conan Exiles to Unreal Engine 5. Taking such a beloved game and updating it with new technology, materials and shaders, UI, and a range of quality-of-life (QoL) improvements creates a new foundation for the next decade of adventures for players around the world.

Conan Exiles Enhanced will introduce stable 60+ FPS gameplay, upgrade the game’s performance on handheld devices, and deliver ‘a deeper level of immersion for both new players and long-time fans.’

The free update, which arrives on Steam on May 5th, also introduces open travel between the Exiled Lands and the Isle of Siptah, for those who own the expansion. Additionally, players will now be able to create multiple characters per account and lean on an enhanced and revamped user interface.

The team at Funcom revealed that your progress in the base game will carry across into the upgraded version, the client size has been slashed by 80 GB, modding support will be available from day one, and crafting and building mechanics have been overhauled.

It’s a massive update.

Are you going to get into Conan Exiles for the Enhanced upgrade? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Lord of Hatred’s intro cinematic can be watched now

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Is Lacrosse 26 On PlayStation, Xbox, PC or Nintendo Switch? All Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
The wait is almost over, as Lacrosse 26 is set to end the eight-year drought of Lacrosse video games. It’s a big moment for the sport, and many fans will hope that it sells well to prove that there’s a market for it, but that begs the question: where can you play Lacrosse 26?

With so many platforms to choose from these days, ranging from consoles to PC, it’s a reasonable question to have, especially when exclusivity is constantly a talking point.

Well, Insider Gaming was able to drop exclusive details surrounding Lacrosse 26, and we can also share which platforms it’ll be available on when the game officially launches—find out for yourself below.

Is Lacrosse 26 On Xbox?

It’s great news for Xbox Lacrosse fans, as Lacrosse 26 will be launching on both the Xbox Series X|S, and you’ll be getting it first.

Practically every sports title makes its way onto consoles, so it’s no surprise that Lacrosse 26 is heading onto Xbox Series X|S. In fact, Xbox fans will be getting Lacrosse 26 first, as there have been some minor difficulties porting it to another popular console…

  • More Insider Gaming: New Lacrosse 26 Details Ahead of April 24 Release

That being said, if you’re still rocking past-gen consoles, like the Xbox One, you won’t be able to play Lacrosse 26, as it’s a current-gen exclusive. It’s certainly that point in the generation now where new releases are leaving the old consoles behind, and it’s no different with Lacrosse 26.

Is Lacrosse 26 On PlayStation?

Lacrosse 26 will be launching on the PlayStation 5, though if you’re picking up the game at launch, you’ll have to wait a bit longer than Xbox fans.

As alluded to above, Crosse Studios ran into some minor problems when readying the game for its PlayStation 5 launch, resulting in a five-day delay. Thankfully, there aren’t any issues beyond that, and the game will be fully playable on the PS5 once it’s up and running.

Is Lacrosse 26 On Nintendo Switch?

Unfortunately for Nintendo fans, Lacrosse 26 will not be playable on either of the Nintendo Switch consoles.

It’s always a shame to see Nintendo miss out on titles, as the hardware often proves to be an extra hurdle for developers to navigate. Still, if Lacrosse 26 is popular enough, there’s always the chance it could wind up on Switch consoles in the future, though that’s pure speculation—as things stand, no word has been given on a Nintendo port.

Is Lacrosse 26 On PC?

This might be a surprise to many of you out there, but Lacrosse 26 will not be launching on PC: it’s a console exclusive.

Unlike the Nintendo Switch, though, the developers have made it clear that a Steam release is most certainly on the cards. However, that will be focused on once they deem the game in a “good place to support it.”

  • More Insider Gaming: Answering Your Questions About Lacrosse 26

That being said, it shouldn’t be too long before it finds its way onto Steam, but for its launch, all eyes are on the console versions.

Where will you be picking up Lacrosse 26? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming platform hubs, check out the following:

  • Invincible VS
  • Forza Horizon 6
  • NTE: Neverness To Everness

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Lacrosse 26 Release Date Countdown & Price, Explained
item.content:encoded:
It’s been an eight-year wait, but a new Lacrosse video game is finally on its way, as Lacrosse 26 draws ever closer to launch. It’s been built from scratch, so expect some changes to the usual gameplay, but if you’re a fan of the sport, it should hopefully be worth the wait.

Insider Gaming had the luxury of revealing world-first details on Lacrosse 26, ranging from its development to gameplay specifics. It’s been a quick turnaround from reveal to release, but it’s almost time to dive back into Lacrosse like never before.

If you’re ready to get involved and want to pick the game up on day one, here’s when you can expect it to release, as well as how much it will set you back.

When Is Lacrosse 26 Coming Out? Release Date, Explained

You won’t have to wait long at all, as Lacrosse 26 is launching on April 24, 2026, on Xbox Series X|S, though it’s a bit later than planned for the PS5 edition.

The game was originally planned to release on April 24, 2026, across both Xbox and PlayStation platforms, but the development team ran into some technical issues during the release process on PS5.

  • More Insider Gaming: First Details On Lacrosse 26’s Gameplay And Franchise

Therefore, Lacrosse 26 has had its launch delayed ever so slightly on the PlayStation console: it will now launch on April 29, 2026, five days after the Xbox release. It’s a slight bummer, especially if you had set the weekend aside for its launch, but thankfully, the wait is nothing drastic, and you’ll be able to pick up on PS5 shortly.

How Much Does Lacrosse 26 Cost? Price, Explained

There’s only one edition of Lacrosse 26 when it launches, and it will set you back $34.99, a price that the development team think is fairly warranted.

  • More Insider Gaming: New Lacrosse 26 Details Ahead of April 24 Release

It’s a stark difference compared to the likes of other sports titles, which commonly launch at the standard $70 price point, but it’s become more and more common to see games go for this mid-range price instead.

Are you excited to finally play a brand new Lacrosse video game? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming release hubs, check out the following:

  • Forza Horizon 6
  • Invincible VS
  • NTE: Neverness To Everness

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
